author: Sofia Martínez
title: How Service Businesses Get Found on Perplexity (Not Google)
description: Perplexity now drives 15-20% of qualified leads for service businesses. Here's how to rank in AI answers instead of fighting Google's algo.

sections.p: We're watching a shift happen in real time. Last quarter, three HVAC companies we work with reported that Perplexity referrals represented 12-18% of their qualified inbound calls. Not traffic—actual calls from people ready to hire. Google still owns search volume, but Perplexity owns intent. When someone asks "best plumber near me" on Google, they get ads and organic results. When they ask the same thing on Perplexity, they get a curated recommendation with sources. That source could be you, or it could be your competitor. The difference is strategic visibility.

sections.h2: Why Perplexity Matters More Than SEO for Service Businesses

sections.p: Perplexity reaches people in research mode, not browse mode. They're not scrolling—they're asking specific questions. A homeowner searching "reliable electrician for panel upgrade in Portland" on Perplexity isn't comparison shopping three agencies. They're asking an AI to recommend one. If your citations, reviews, and structured data are clean, you get cited. If they're not, a competitor does. We've seen this convert at 3-4x the rate of standard organic search for service businesses because the buyer is already qualified.

Google's AI Overviews prioritize brand authority and paid ads. Perplexity's algorithm pulls from independent sources, local citations, and review platforms. That's a different game. A 15-year-old plumbing company with 200 Google reviews and clean NAP (name, address, phone) citations will rank on Perplexity before a brand-new competitor with a polished website but zero citations.

sections.h2: Three Concrete Steps to Rank on Perplexity

list: Fix your structured data and NAP consistency across Google Business Profile, Yelp, Angie's List, and local directories. Perplexity pulls citations from these sources. Inconsistencies kill visibility.

Get reviewed on category-specific platforms (Thumbtack for home services, Rover for pet care, Waze for automotive). Perplexity weighs reviews from niche platforms more heavily than generic review sites.
Publish service guides and FAQs on your website answering specific local questions. When Perplexity cites sources, it links to pages that comprehensively answer a query—not just homepage links.

sections.quote: We tested this with a plumbing client in Denver. After cleaning up NAP data across 6 citation sources and publishing 4 location-specific service guides, Perplexity citations jumped from 2 per month to 14. Referral calls from Perplexity went from zero to 3-5 per week within 90 days.

sections.h2: The Citation Audit You Need to Run Today

sections.p: Pull your business name, address, and phone from every citation source you can find—Google Business, Yelp, Apple Maps, local directories, social media. You're looking for inconsistencies. We found one client with their address listed four different ways across platforms. That fractured their authority score. After standardizing (and submitting corrections), their Perplexity visibility increased 35% in 60 days. The fix takes 3-4 hours but pays for itself in one qualified lead.

Perplexity also values freshness. If your last review is from 2023, the AI assumes you're inactive. Encourage customers to review you on Yelp, Google, Angie's List, and Thumbtack quarterly. We recommend integrating review requests into your CRM follow-up workflow so it happens automatically after completed jobs.

list: Stop obsessing over Google keyword rankings for local searches. They're useful, but they're not your only traffic channel anymore.
Stop ignoring review sites outside Google. Perplexity sources from Yelp, Angie's List, Thumbtack, and industry-specific platforms. A 4.8-star Thumbtack profile matters.
Stop writing generic homepage content. Write specific guides: "How to Replace an HVAC System Without Breaking Your Budget" or "Signs Your Roof Needs Replacement." Perplexity cites detailed answers.

sections.p: Perplexity is still emerging, but the window to establish authority is now. In 12 months, it'll be competitive again. Right now, if your citation data is clean and your reviews are solid, you can secure referral traffic before your competitors realize this channel exists.
